meaning: adj.气馁的,悲哀的,沮丧的,(眼睛)向下看的
examples: She was silent, her eyes downcast.
She was so downcast, so afraid that it should die!
The people observe thy downcast head, thy clouded mien, and they take it for an omen.
Despite losing their eighth successive Test match in Australia, West Indies were not too downcast.
Coal exporters are downcast, as they also face low global prices for the black stuff.
